ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Mar-07-2024 Off Calendar

Application For An Order To Show Cause Re Contempt Against William Cotter
Off Calendar Nov-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PETITIONER GOODLEAP, LLC's Application For An Order To Show Cause Re Contempt Against William Cotter.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: JUDGMENT CREDITOR GOODLEAP, LLC'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "JUDMENT CREIDITOR" OR "GOODLEAP" UNOPPOSED APPLICATION FOR AN ORDER TO SHOW RE: CONTEMPT AGAINST WILLIAM COTTER (HEREAFTER "COTTER) IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS COTTER IN CONTEMPT OF THIS COURT'S ORDER OF JULY 13, 2023 AND IMPOSES PUNISHMENT IN THE AMOUNT OF TWO THOUSAND DOLLARS ($2,000.00) PLUS REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS OF FOUR THOUSAND, SEVEN HUNDRES AND TWENTY DOLLARS ($4,720.00).

COUNSEL FOR GOODLEAP SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER, SUBMIT A COPY TO COTTER FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY TO THE COURT (rennepar85@gmail.com) IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING ON NOVEMBER 21, 2023. THE DELIVERY OF THE PROPOSED ORDER SHOULD NOT AWAIT APPROVAL BY COTTER.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported (302/RBU)

COUNSEL FOR GOODLEAP SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER, SUBMIT A COPY TO SUNSTOR FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORDFORM TO THE COURT (rennepar85@gmail.com) IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING ON NOVEMBER 21, 2023. THE DELIVERY OF THE PROPOSED ORDER SHOULD NOT AWAIT APPROVAL BY SUNSTOR.

Atty. IAN K. MCGLONE, Esq. of Boutin Jones Inc. (Tel: 916-321-4444) for plaintiff/judgment creditor GoodLeap, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF GOODLEAP, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENAS.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for plaintiff (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The unopposed motion is granted.

Prevailing party to send a proposed order in Word format to jpdeol@dhillonlaw.com.
